title: Paper Editor fixes: correct question insertion, inline bank editing, and side-by-side match/table layouts

requirements.summary: Insert a full deep-copied Question object from Question Bank into the target paper section with a new id and correct headingId.
acceptanceCriteria: From the Paper Editor flow (using the insert context), clicking “Use” on a Question Bank item adds a new question to the chosen paper section and the user is navigated back to the Real Paper editor.
The inserted question is a deep copy of the source question’s structure/content (including options and images), except for a new unique id.
The inserted question appears in the editor with the same question type and renders correctly (MCQ, match-pairs, table, etc.).

If the destination section has headings, the inserted question is assigned to the section’s active/first heading (do not leave headingId null if headings exist), so it is visible under that heading in the PaperSurface list.

file_operations.description: Add reusable helpers to (1) deep-clone a Question, (2) generate a new unique question id, (3) compute destination headingId based on the destination section’s headings (use first heading when headings exist), and (4) return updated sections with inserted questions for the insert context.

Fix the Question Bank “Use” action to insert a full Question object (not just text/marks). Use the new insertion helper to deep-copy the selected question (including mcqOptions/fillInBlankData/trueFalseData/matchPairsData/tableData/imageAttachment and any rich cell content/images), assign a new id, set headingId appropriately for the destination section, update the paper in the mock store, clear the insert context, and navigate back to the Real Paper editor. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Make AI “Add to Paper” reliably insert full Question objects into the section indicated by insert context, with headingId assignment and a clear error when context is missing.
acceptanceCriteria: When navigating from a paper editor to the AI Assistant with an insert context set, selecting generated questions and clicking “Add to Paper” adds them to the correct paper section and returns to the Real Paper editor.
Inserted AI questions appear under the correct section heading (headingId set when headings exist).
No insertion occurs when there is no insert context; the UI shows a clear error message and does not crash.

file_operations.description: Refactor “Add to Paper” to use the shared insertion helper so inserted items are valid full Question objects (with correct questionType, new unique ids, and headingId set to the destination section’s first heading when headings exist). Replace bare alert-only handling for missing insert context with a clear inline error message in the page UI and prevent any insertion attempts. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Extend helper to support inserting multiple questions at once (AI selection) while ensuring each inserted Question receives a unique id and consistent headingId assignment for the destination section.

requirements.summary: Enable inline editing for personal questions in Question Bank and persist edits to the personal question store.
acceptanceCriteria: In the Question Bank “My Questions” tab, clicking “Edit” toggles the question card into an editable state (e.g., textarea/input) without navigating away.
Edits can be saved (e.g., via a Save/Done control or by toggling Edit off) and are persisted using the existing store update method so they remain after refresh.
Cancelling/discarding edits (if provided) does not mutate stored data.
Editing does not break the “Use” button behavior.

file_operations.description: Implement inline edit mode for “My Questions” cards: add local UI state per item (editing/draft text), render an inline input/textarea when editing, add Save/Cancel (or Done/Discard) controls, and persist via useMockStore.updatePersonalQuestion. Ensure “Use” continues to insert via the same full-object insertion flow and is unaffected by editing state. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Ensure match-pairs Column A and Column B are always strictly side-by-side in both editor and renderer, using horizontal scrolling on small screens instead of stacking.
acceptanceCriteria: For match-pairs questions, Column A and Column B remain in a two-column layout at all viewport widths (no stacking).
On small screens, the pair area remains usable (e.g., allows horizontal scroll within the question block) without breaking the rest of the page layout.
Both editor mode and read/print renderer mode reflect the side-by-side requirement consistently.

file_operations.description: Update match-pairs rendering layout to never collapse to a single column (remove responsive stacking). Wrap the two-column area in an internal horizontal scroll container and enforce a minimum width for the two columns so they stay side-by-side on narrow screens while not breaking the overall page layout. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Update match-pairs editor layout to remain two columns at all viewport widths by applying an internal horizontal scroll container/min-width constraints around the two-column grid, ensuring the editor remains usable on mobile without stacking. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add/adjust print-safe CSS utilities/classes used by match-pairs to support fixed side-by-side layout and internal horizontal scrolling without affecting the rest of the paper layout.

requirements.summary: Improve table question UI with editable Column A/Column B headers and darker, print-safe borders in editor and renderer.
acceptanceCriteria: Table questions render visible headers labeled “Column A” and “Column B” by default.
Users can edit the header text inline; changes persist in the question’s table data model and are reflected in both editor and renderer views.
Table borders are visually darker than before, with the outside border and header separators also rendered in dark border styling.
The styling works in both normal view and print view (no missing borders when printing).

file_operations.description: Extend the table question data model to store editable column header text (defaulting to “Column A”/“Column B” when not present) in a backward-compatible way for existing saved papers/questions.

Update table editor to render a header row above the table cells showing editable Column A/Column B header inputs, persisting edits into the question’s tableData header fields via onUpdate. Apply darker border styling classes for inner gridlines and an obvious outer border. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Update table renderer to show a header row (default “Column A”/“Column B” if unset) and apply darker border styling (inner + outer) that remains visible in print. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add/adjust print-safe table CSS (darker borders, strong outer border, header separators) used by both renderer and editor table markup, ensuring consistent appearance in print view.

requirements.summary: Reduce excessive whitespace in table cells containing images so cell height better fits image/content while keeping image controls functional.
acceptanceCriteria: After inserting/pasting an image into a table cell, the cell does not expand to an unnecessarily tall area; whitespace above/below the image is minimized.
The image remains visible and controllable (resize/alignment controls still work).
The change does not regress text editing within the same cell and does not break print rendering of table cell images.

file_operations.description: Adjust RichCellEditor layout behavior inside table cells to avoid unnecessary vertical growth when images exist (e.g., reduce default textarea vertical footprint, tighten wrapper spacing, and ensure image wrappers don’t impose large minimum heights) while preserving existing image resize/alignment controls. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Tune CSS for rich cell containers/images within table cells to minimize extra whitespace (tight margins/padding, avoid excessive min-heights) and keep print rendering stable.

Ensure table cell rich content rendering aligns to top and uses the updated rich-cell/table styles so images don’t create excessive blank space in read/print modes. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
